prose.en: When transferring information between different security domains, implement a linear content filter pipeline that is enforced with discretionary and mandatory access controls.

guidance.en: Content filtering is the process of inspecting information as it traverses a cross-domain solution and determines if the information meets a predefined policy. The use of linear content filter pipelines ensures that filter processes are non-bypassable and always invoked. In general, the use of parallel filtering architectures for content filtering of a single data type introduces bypass and non-invocation issues.

prose.en: when transferring information between security domains, a linear content filter pipeline is implemented that is enforced with discretionary and mandatory access controls.

objects.en: system configuration settings and associated documentation
Organizational personnel with information flow enforcement responsibilities
organizational personnel with information security responsibilities
Mechanisms implementing information flow enforcement functions
